title: HED Schema Browser help
intro: HED (Hierarchical Event Descriptors) is a framework for annotating time-series data such as experimental events using a standardized vocabulary (a HED schema). This tool lets you browse the HED standard and library schemas. All schema data is read live from the GitHub hed-standard/hed-schemas repository, so it always reflects the published schemas.

sections.heading: Choosing a schema
items: Use the first dropdown to select the standard schema or a library schema (for example score, lang, or testlib).
Use the Version dropdown to pick a released version. Turn on 'Show deprecated' to also list older, deprecated versions.
'Show prerelease' switches to the in-development (prerelease) version of the selected schema, when one exists. 'Show deprecated' is not available while viewing a prerelease.
'Show library only' hides the inherited standard-schema tags so you see only the tags a library adds.

sections.heading: Browsing the tag tree
items: The left panel shows the schema as a collapsible tree of tags.

Click the caret before a parent tag to show or hide its children: a right-pointing caret means collapsed, a down-pointing caret means expanded. From the keyboard, focus a tag and press the Right or Left arrow.
'Expand all' opens the entire tree; 'Expand to level' opens the tree to a chosen depth.
Type in the 'Search term' box to jump to a matching tag. Turn on 'Search deprecated tags' to include deprecated tags in the search.

sections.heading: Viewing tag details
items: Hover a tag to preview its description, attributes, long-form path, and hedId in the 'Tag details' panel on the right.
Click a tag name to pin its details in the panel. You can pin several tags at once to compare them side by side.
Remove one pinned tag with its close (x) button, or remove them all with 'Clear all'.

sections.heading: Schema extra sections
items: Below the tree, 'Schema extra sections' lists the schema's unit classes, unit modifiers, value classes, schema attributes, properties, sources, prefixes, and external annotations.
Use 'Show descriptions' to reveal each entry's description inline, and 'Show attributes' to reveal its attributes.

sections.heading: Adjusting the layout
items: Drag the vertical divider between the tree and the details panel to change how much width each one gets.
Drag the horizontal bar below the tree, or below the extra sections, to change how tall those areas are. Each area has its own scrollbar.

links.label: HED schemas repository (source of all schema data)
